A rare mineral with unique energy properties. It is highly sought after by the Ferengi, who consider it a valuable commodity. Its exact properties are unknown, but it is believed to have applications in highly experimental energy generation and weapons development.

Armor that can absorb and dissipate energy weapons. The technology was developed by Starfleet and is considered one of the most advanced forms of armor in the galaxy. It is highly effective against energy weapons but less effective against physical attacks.

Originally traded by the Orion Syndicate and banned by the Federation, the Orion Seismic Charges are able of creating a gravimetric gradient similar to that of a collapsed dwarf star. It is a highly volatile and destructive tool, capable of causing huge amounts of collateral damage to both ecosystems and enemies in its proximity.

These weapons are torpedoes that can pass through shields and cause massive damage to ships. The technology was developed by Starfleet and was used to destroy a Borg transwarp hub. Its use is strictly regulated due to the risk of collateral damage.

This subatomic technology native to the Hirogen is capable of making quick scans of other vessels and heavily disrupting a ship’s weaponry and navigation systems. It is known to be used by Hirogen Hunters, combined with a tractor beam, to neutralize and capture their prey.

Armor made of nanoprobes that can repair damage and adapt to new threats. The technology was developed by the Borg and is considered one of the most advanced forms of armor in the galaxy. The crew of the U.S.S. Voyager pioneered use of this technology by grafting some plating onto their ship’s hull.

Proven to be a highly efficient resource extraction tool, the Romulan Mining Laser emits a high-energy pulse capable of drilling through any planet's crust. Due to the huge power of its beam, it is believed it can disrupt transporters and subspace communications.

Extraction tool used by the Malon Export Vessels to handle vast amounts of extremely hazardous antimatter waste. Since the Malon lack the technology to recycle antimatter, the load on these vessels is often disposed of in open space in order to avoid the emission of theta radiation.
A powerful drilling device used by the Klingons to extract valuable resources from planets. The technology is highly advanced and capable of penetrating even the toughest planetary crusts.

A device used by the Borg to regenerate and assimilate new technology. The alcove is essential to the Borg's ability to adapt and evolve, and it allows them to quickly integrate new knowledge and abilities into their collective consciousness. Under strict supervision, a modified alcove can be integrated into a ship’s main computer to improve the accuracy of weapons systems and targeting scanners. This extremely dangerous upgrade runs the risk of assimilating the host ship and is considered a reckless measure.

A cloaking device that allows the ship to pass through solid objects. The technology was developed by the Tholians and is considered one of the rarest and most coveted technologies in the galaxy. Due to the unstable nature of Interphase Space, this advancement is considered highly dangerous.

A highly dangerous and lethal form of radiation that can destroy all organic matter. It was developed by Shinzon of Remus and used as a portable weapon to eradicate the Romulan Senate. This is considered one of the deadliest weapons in the galaxy. Its use is banned by the United Federation of Planets.

A powerful and dangerous substance that has the ability to create a black hole. The technology was developed by the Vulcan Science Academy and was used by Spock in an attempt to stop a supernova from destroying the Romulan homeworld. Its use is strictly regulated due to the risk of catastrophic consequences.

A rare and precious crystal that has the power to manipulate time. The technology is so advanced that only a handful of civilizations in the galaxy have ever been able to harness its power. It is said that possession of a Time Crystal is the key to unlocking the secrets of the universe.

A weapon developed by the Haakonian Order, the Metreon Cascade is a devastating explosive that releases highly unstable metreon isotopes upon detonation. It has the power to destroy entire planets and is considered one of the most dangerous weapons in the galaxy.

Control, one of the most advanced AI beings in space, was once a key member of Starfleet, modelling Section 31's threat assessments, before betraying Starfleet and massacring the crew of Section 31's HQ. The Seeker Probe is one of Control's many eyes, guided by its intelligence and malice, but when this eye looks at you, you're dead.

The Ralnag have a saying: two guns are better than one. It is an ethos that lies at the heart of their weapon design. No firearm has just one barrel, no knife a single blade, and the Ralnag's warheads are no exception. Designed to inflict double the destruction and double the pain, the Dual Photon Warheads form a weapon of exquisite cruelty.

After the destruction of Vulcan in 2258, Section 31 supervised the development of a long range weapon originally devised by Khan Noonien Singh. Breathtaking in their destructive capabilities, these weapons are very much the children of the cold, warped intelligence that birthed them.

When a species exists in a perpetual state of war, all science, all endeavor, is guided towards one goal only: the utter annihilation of the enemy. Brutal weapons are constructed to inflict maximum damage, and the Hull Piercer is the perfect example, cutting through its foes like a sword of fire through wax. A pity its creators turned it against each other when their war was won.

Created by the Druoda, a species known for their AI-controlled long range weapons, this warhead is a thinking entity containing bio-neural circuitry. The Sentient Missile is a fearsome weapon of mass destruction, frighteningly singleminded in its terrible function of raining down annihilation.

Discovered drifting amongst the debris of a destroyed system on the outermost edge of the Alpha Quadrant, this darkly intricate component was found to contain a psychotic AI personality matrix. When plugged into a ship's systems, the Berserker Complex takes over the vessel entirely, transforming it into a terrifyingly unpredictable tool of terror.

Cybernetic implants are rarely used within the Federation, though they have contributed greatly to medical advancement in recent decades. This particular model of implant has been callously resequenced to manipulate its wearer. With further testing, the implant could be weaponized to neutralize key personnel and turn the tide of battle while boarding target vessels.

This devious invention is most frequently used to steal goods from unsecured cargo holds. The Orion Syndicate have configured this transporter to bypass certain shield frequencies, but with enhanced risk that each transport attempt will fail, leaving anything - or anyone - involved reduced to phased-out atoms.

This hand-held weapon is capable of discharging a concussive energy pulse when lashed, stunning or injuring anyone in the line of fire. Ferengi warriors typically rely on the Energy Whip when on away missions. Ferengi weapons dealers have been marketing the weapon and have built up a lucrative black market distribution network, despite the weapons having been banned by the wider galactic community.

The highly volatile Breen Energy Dampener uses a complex array of suppressive radiation bands to disrupt and neutralize energy-based technology. When used effectively, it can disrupt the shields and critical computer systems of enemy ships. Despite the Breen’s best efforts, the device is quite unstable and uses an astronomical amount of energy to operate.

Now stored in the Daystrom Institute on Earth in the “Self-Aware Megalomaniacal Computer Storage” annex, AGIMUS previously coerced an entire planet into a century-long war, amongst other atrocities. The device is considered a Priority 1 security threat, with the potential to take control of networks of any size.

This isolytic weapon was designed by the brilliant Ruon Tarka, a Risian scientist, despite such technology being banned since the Second Khitomer Accords. Tarka built the weapon against the Federation’s orders in an attempt to neutralize the Dark Matter Anomaly. Though Tarka’s plan failed, his schematics have been illicitly repurposed for use on starships. Tarka’s Isolytic Weapon generates a cascading subspace burst that will destroy the user’s enemies...  And potentially cause irreparable damage to subspace itself.

The Tantalus Field is a rare piece of technology from the Mirror Universe, kept well in secret by those who gained control over it. This small device proved pivotal in Captain James T. Kirk's career, saving him trouble from dealing with opponents and competitors by simply erasing them from existence. While the device's origins are unknown since it was plundered from the laboratory of an alien scientist, it found itself incredibly useful in a world where having no enemies means getting rid of all of them.
With several adjustments, the area of the Tantalus Field's effect can be applied to a group of people or even a whole enemy ship. The device will calculate all the weaknesses and vulnerabilities to turn even the mightiest foe into a petty small fry, subject to domination and immediate elimination. Such power is sought after by far too many, which makes the Tantalus Field a technology that is not just forbidden but hunted for; better keep it close before becoming the next in line of mysterious disappearances.

When diplomacy and friendship don't provide the expected results, it might be worth changing your tactics from bringing love to evoking fear. Agony Booth, a novel and trusty invention of the Terran Empire, will quickly subdue your enemies and make them obedient servants, saving hours and hours of doing favors and appealing to strangers' interests.
Unlike the Agonizer, its previous step of evolution, the Booth has perfected the art of subduction by stimulating neural centers, synaptic scan calibrating it for each species, personal pain tolerance levels, and the required survivability times, as well as shifting the stimulation for constant excruciating anguish. With targeted use and various settings, nobody will remain indifferent to your words and actions, and the fear of pain will only make your "friends" more loyal and forward-going (side effects may include dissident ideas, which are corrected by additional exposure to Agony Booth).

A highly advanced form of propulsion technology, the quantum slipstream drive essentially renders the term ‘maximum warp’ meaningless. It was discovered in the Delta Quadrant by the crew of the U.S.S. Voyager. Created by a Delta Quadrant race the Borg designated Species 116, the quantum slipstream drive works like Borg transwarp technology, allowing a ship to travel well beyond a starship’s maximum warp. But be warned, there are times when it is possible to move too fast, so fast you might even leave yourself behind.

Despite its blissful outward appearance and omnipotent population, the extra-dimensional realm of the Q Continuum is far from an idyllic paradise. Immortality has caused every member of the Continuum to face an eternal enemy - boredom. Throughout their history, all locations and all timelines were explored, all topics discussed, and all stories told. To the average Q, a universe full of possibilities could be seen as mundane and empty as a desert road. Quinn, a prominent Continuum philosopher, speculated regarding the purpose of his own being when he witnessed Q losing his immortality at the hands of his fellow Q. By experiencing life anew as a mere mortal, Q led Quinn to a scandalous idea - ending his own existence. After having taken asylum aboard the U.S.S. Voyager and turning into a mortal, Quinn set his plan into motion. This death caused a stir in a previously tranquil Continuum, leading to a full-on Civil War between two factions: conservatives, willing to keep the order of things as it has been for millennia, and the Freedom Faction, fighting for individualism and personal freedom. Even though this war appeared as a simple human conflict, the consequences of this war rippled throughout the Galaxy. One of the most disturbing outcomes led to the development of a weapon capable of killing a Q, bypassing their immortality and apparent invulnerability. Too complex to be comprehended by mortal eyes, the weapon took the form of a cannon commonly utilized during the American Civil War. However, its power and influence are impossible to measure. Even storing this cannon onboard an Independent station retains the capacity to disturb the whole existence of the Universe. A human mind is yet too fragile to process the consequences of using such a weapon.

While the concept of time travel is not unheard of, it has never been easy. It was a complex, energy-intensive, and somewhat crude process until the 29th century. However, one piece of 29th-century technology was not only sneaked into the past but completely reconstructed from scratch. When a group of Na'kuhl militants escaped defeat in their timeline and reached Earth in 1944, their main goal was to return to their time and restart the war they almost lost. In order to execute this plan, Vosk, the leader of the Na'kuhl group, used the resources of the Axis powers and reconstructed the Temporal Conduit. This very same technology was deployed in the future in order to help the Na’kuhl escape into the past. However, this primitive iteration was enormous in size, less stable, and an overall fragile piece of technology. When Captain Archer of the Enterprise NX-01 prevented Vosk from traveling to the 29th Century, the Temporal Conduit, a technology from a now-destroyed timeline, should have ceased to exist... And yet, it didn’t. Controlling the flow of time itself is immensely dangerous and gives anyone wielding this technology the power to predict their enemy’s moves, thoughts, or even their impending existence. As modern technologies have allowed the Temporal Conduit to shrink to the size of a warp drive, it can now be utilized aboard a starship, allowing its wielder the ability to use time itself as a weapon against their foes.

It’s one thing to make your opponent accommodate your needs. But it’s much better if your opponent wants to serve you and follow your wishes. That is the very purpose of the Thought Maker. Although studies on this technology’s inner workings are still in the preliminary stages, the Thought Maker’s effects upon deployment are widely known: it alters the subject’s mind and dampens their memories, making anyone under its influence extremely susceptible to suggestions and new ideas. Incredible in its potential for destruction, the use of the Thought Maker is expressly forbidden within the Ferengi Alliance, and its use for achieving prosperity and income is frowned upon. And yet it was a Ferengi, Bok, who purchased this piece of technology to use on one Captain Jean-Luc Picard. In an attempt to avenge his departed son, Bok almost compelled Captain Picard into destroying U.S.S. Enterprise-D, but was stopped just in time. Dealing with enemies has never been easier with the Thought Maker in your arsenal. It does not just provide an insight into the motives and next moves of your enemy - it’s a tool to make their next step yours to command.

The ren-faire inspired world of Hysperia may seem harmless, but woe betides those who would dare interrupt the Hysperian way of life. Royal smiths, alchemists, and even lowly bards aren’t letting any potential enemies ruin their idyllic world. Messing up a fiend’s communication is the key to confounding enemy tactics, and what could be a better way to hide an electromagnetic disruptor than in an unassuming, “harmless” lute?
Hidden in plain sight, the Electromagnetic Lute can be used easily at any moment as long as there remains someone with the required skills to play it. After striking a chord, enemies are cut off from communicating with their allies. Striking a follow-up chord results in all hostile electronic devices being rendered useless. Play an entire melody, and your enemies will inevitably know defeat.
Of course, for the ambitious Hysperians, such technologies are merely a first step in establishing dominance on the battlefield. With enough modifications, the Electromagnetic Lute's effects can be enhanced to spread through the vacuum of space and target not only crew members, but entire vessels and even armadas. The ballad of Queen Paolana must sound in every corner of the Universe, and as long as Hysperia stands, the Royal bards shall play on.

The brightest minds of the Hysperian people created wonders such as the Monaveen and its "magical" components, which are in no way inferior to Starfleet's prime vessels. The Hysperian people of the Mirror Universe have gone even further, employing all sorts of "dark" and "forbidden" knowledge to survive and prosper in this vast and dangerous dimension. To foresee the enemy's tactics and strip them of all of their glory, the engineers of the royal court tested the limits of their technology for centuries. Finally, they invented the infamous Interphasic Mirror - a device boasting Renaissance faire authenticity while remaining immensely valuable in protecting the ever-flourishing lands of Hysperia.

The frame of the Mirror consists of intricate mechanisms produced by the most advanced blacksmiths of the realm, prognosticating every fiend's move and seeking their weak points. A novel inclusion of augmented antimatter components allows the Interphasic Mirror to gather a portion of Isolytic Damage and weave it onto the ship's offense, borrowing power from the foes and ensuring their inevitable - and painful - demise. Exclusive to the Hysperian fleet, the Interphasic Mirror is a unique piece of art, elevating the owner's might and dominance in the battlefield with Renaissance style.

"An experimental compound with destructive powers capable of collapsing stars, Trilithium is a nuclear inhibitor with an insidious reputation. Originally developed within the borders of the Romulan Star Empire, the cunning sisters of the House of Duras managed to steal its stabilized version for Dr. Tolian Soran, an El-Aurian scientist who wished to utilize the Trilithium in a weapon that would destroy entire star systems, thus moving a great energy ribbon acting as a doorway to the Nexus to a desired point in space. While Soran’s plans were eventually thwarted by Starfleet Captains Jean-Luc Picard and James T. Kirk, the schematics of the trilithium-based weapon survived and proliferated throughout the black markets.
Trilithium’s devastating force lies in its potential application as a weapon of mass destruction. When utilized within the warhead of a ballistic missile, it is capable of wiping out entire solar systems... along with anyone unlucky enough to remain within the vicinity. A disturbing by-product of its usage, trilithium resin, is known to be a highly toxic substance for humans. Even a sample of four-hundred kilograms can render a planet uninhabitable."

"Even the most seemingly perfect society can be built upon the foundations of a terrible secret. In the case of the Enarans, their peaceful colonies only remain peaceful through subjugation. When the deployment of new technologies such as long-distance communications interlinks and microfusion generators gave rise to this highly-developed society, technophobic Enarans who denied this progress in favor of simple lives and antiquated traditions were deemed a danger to the rest of Enaran society. The Enarans would cast these “Regressives” into exile beyond the city walls, and would continue to survey their lives day and night. However, when this level of control wasn’t enough, the technologically progressive cohort of their society created Enaran Execution Posts. With this fearsome new technology, they managed to get rid of all the undesirable “Regressives” in a swift and brutal manner.
Execution Posts are simple yet highly effective in their role. When the prisoner is securely restrained, an executioner remotely activates the Post, and it engulfs the unfortunate subject in a purple glow. In as little as four seconds, the prisoner’s life is over, and so are Enaran's troubles. Performed in public, these executions have become not only a practical tool to deal with enemies of progress, but also stand as a symbol of punishment, control, and a price of peace for the rest of the population. With a little creative application, the Enaran Execution Post can be a formidable weapon in the right hands."

The Borg are known to collect and assimilate the technology of any species they’ve encountered. However, in rare instances, these roles reverse, and the Collective’s technology can be snatched away and used by others. Unimatrix Shielding - a sliver of the vast arsenal of the Borg, is one such technology that eventually fell into the hands of the U.S.S. Voyager crew. Lieutenant Commander Tuvok repurposed this defensive technology in order to protect a multi-spatial probe from the aggressive atmosphere of a Class 6 gas giant, while his colleague Tom Paris used it to perfect the newly constructed Delta Flyer. A replica of the Borg’s original technology, the Unimatrix Shielding quickly adapts to incoming damage, and nullifies energy weaponry deployed by any hostile - a perfect fit for a shuttle designed to navigate the potentially hazardous environments of the Delta Quadrant. In an ironic twist of fate, the original Delta Flyer was destroyed by the Borg on a mission to assist the drones of Unimatrix Zero. However, in the wake of this unfortunate incident, a new Delta Flyer was constructed, and Unimatrix Shielding remained a studied and understood technology in the hands of Starfleet.

"The Multiphasic Chamber has been created and constructed by the crew of U.S.S. Equinox in a desperate attempt to gather energy from nucleogenic lifeforms, which is used for augmenting the warp drive in order to expedite the Equinox’s journey back to Earth. This device would surround a nucleogenic creature with a multiphasic force field, preventing it from escaping the normal spacetime continuum via interspatial fissures. However, having learned that the caged lifeforms could be converted into an energy source, the Multiphasic Chamber was modified with a polaron grid and a submolecular resequencer, allowing it to convert the alien cell structures into a crystalline compound. It remains difficult to speculate how would the journey of the Voyager would have been altered if Captain Ransom’s ideas were taken aboard, but the principles of Captain Janeway are unwavering, leading to the destruction of Equinox and the Multiphasic Chambers along with it. But as long as nucleogenic lifeforms exist and as long as there are desperate vessels stranded in the far corners of the Universe, the Multiphasic Chamber will power warp engines despite the questionable ethics of its usage."

The Chronophages, otherwise known as time bugs, are a terrifying creation of the Krenim. Developed with the eerie ability to disrupt one's flow through the timestream, one "bite" from a time bug is enough to freeze you in place instantly... and permanently.

Designed and developed by the Orion Syndicate, using the shattered remnants of a Krenim ship, the Chrono Deflector is a uniquely powerful, and hazardous, piece of technology. 

The Chrono Deflector builds up Tachyonic radiation every time the vessel takes a direct hit to its polarized hull, channeling the kinetic energy into potential energy. That potential energy can then be used to empower the ship's systems to an obscene degree. The Syndicate boasts that, of the dozen or so field tests they conducted, a mere 24% of vessels using the Chrono Deflector were ripped apart in a quantum implosion. 

Well worth the extra firepower, they say.

The Emperor's regalia are forged and displayed to demonstrate the current ruler's sheer power and might over subjects and enemies. But above every tyrant hangs the sword of Damocles, and no true monarch will thrive without being ready to endure potential betrayal.
All Terran Emperors know the price of their position too well, and nobody wishes to abdicate their throne without putting up a fight. That's why Philippa Georgiou commissioned a secret weapon hidden from commoners' sight - the Godsend. This deceptively simple device is a last resort meant to eviscerate the dissidents who would dare to harm the monarch. Upon detonation, the device is capable of laying waste to the equivalent area of a quadrant - a cataclysmic event powerful enough to evaporate all lifeforms unfortunate enough to find themselves within the blast radius. Seeing the potential catastrophe if the device is ever used, Emperor Georgiou ordered the destruction of the Godsend, only to learn years later that her order was never executed.
Even the detonation hasn't erased The Godsend from this world, and it was soon replicated by Section 31can be considered a last resort in the most dire predicaments, enhancing the power of your weapons and compromising the defensive capabilities of your foes. The reputation of this ultimate weapon has been fueled by rumors of its destructive potential - the mere possibility of its presence can end a conflict before it starts. Following its acquisition from the Mirror Universe by Section 31, its presence endangers the lives of many innocent people, and if it falls into the wrong hands, their world as they know it could cease to exist.

Section 31 always seeks information: intercepted transmissions, bugged communications, anonymous tip-offs, and a vast chain of spy operations. But when powerful enemies are breathing down your neck, having every bit of information is insufficient - you must know their future steps. Control, as its name suggests, granted the organization just that. The threat assessment system powered by artificial intelligence was created to bring universal peace, a tool to prevent future wars and suppress hostilities before they even start. And yet, Control almost caused a complete wipeout of any sentient life, stopped only by the brave actions of the Discovery and the U.S.S. Enterprise crews. The agents of Section 31 made all possible efforts to eliminate anything remaining of Control from existence, but the ideas can’t be killed that easily. A system that can predict a move before the thought of it is even conceived is a tipping point in the universal power race, and despite the dangers artificial intelligence core entails, everyone craves to get Control.

A field of magnetic tetrahedron Tothian mines almost annihilated the NSEA Protector - until it became their greatest weapon. Jason Nesmith’s crew, inexperienced and lacking conviction, first encountered it while seeking shelter from Sarris: they managed to escape at the cost of severe damage to the ship.  However, that encounter didn’t deter the crew the second time. After confronting Sarris once again, Jason Nesmith ordered to head right into the minefield, taking advantage of Tommy Webber’s skills and a deeper knowledge of the mines’ potential to gather them behind the Protector and, in a sleek maneuver, blow up Sarris’s vessel, claiming victory for the crew.
The origin of this minefield remains a mystery, yet some claim it’s an artifact of the past left after a great war long ago. Time hasn’t affected the capacity of the mines, as they are still capable of causing massive damage to any vessel and, when gathered together, can tear through the defenses of the sturdiest battleships. Their unique geometry provides protection against incoming damage and deals critical blows to the hostiles, and magnetic properties allow the mines to collect the remains of the unlucky ones for effective loot sourcing - a great weapon for a great war.

"If you need a little something special, be it for one target, or multiple targets, we got it, you'll see it, here on Minos, where we live by the motto, 'Peace through superior firepower'." Echo Papa is the last relic of the Minosians and their most outstanding - or most catastrophic - achievement. The people of Minos were known as prominent arms dealers who excelled at military research and salesmanship. However, at one point in history, the whole population mysteriously disappeared. When the U.S.S. Enterprise-D set out to investigate the U.S.S. Drake’s fate, they were shocked to discover that the Minosian civilization was annihilated by a simple demonstration of their latest invention, and only the wits of Picard and Beverly Crusher managed to stop the onslaught. With a single drone capable of severely damaging the Enterprise-D, a legion of them could effectively substitute the military powers of an empire. Echo Papa isn’t just firepower - its capabilities include planning attacks, taking prisoners, gathering intelligence, and progressive adaptability. Lost drones are substituted in minutes and continue pursuing their goal relentlessly until their target is destroyed or the Echo Papa system is purchased. Perhaps just one unit of this system remains after the terrible demise of the Minosians. Perhaps this is one unit too many.

The Dominion War remains one of the darkest times in the Alpha and Gamma Quadrants’ history. It cost millions of lives and thousands of ships, but worst of all, it caused the most valiant and heroic people to turn to their darkest sides. The Federation was at the front of the war, and with the chances for a brighter future diminishing fast with the Dominion's advance, some people decided to take matters into their own hands, no matter the cost. Section 31 works in the interest of the Federation, but where the front has to support utopian ideals and the principles of justice, the rogue agents of it don’t hesitate to act dirty. The Morphogenic Virus is the pinnacle of their ideas. 
Planning to end the war fast, Section 31 agents created a biological weapon capable of wiping out the Founders by destroying their cellular structure more and more with every shapeshift. It worked, causing chaos and panic in the enemy’s midst. All the more ironic that it was the cure for the Morphogenic Virus that helped to end the war: Julian Bashir went against Section 31’s orders and created the medicine to stop the degenerative disease. Odo healed the Female Changeling, leading to the Founders’ surrender. And yet, the question remains - could this have been Section 31’s plan all along?

Project Genesis: the means to create life from lifelessness. A process whereby molecular structure is reorganised at the subatomic level into life-generating matter of equal mass. As a number of worlds faced critical issues associated with overpopulation and dwindling natural resources, this groundbreaking endeavor was proposed and developed in response to these challenges. Spearheaded by Dr. Carol Marcus and her son, David Marcus, their vision was realized thanks to the diligent work of some of the brightest scientific minds in the Federation. Through utilization of the Genesis Device, this innovative technology could be deployed on any given lifeless space body. Through an instantaneous reaction known as the “Genesis Effect”, barren moons could theoretically be turned into M-Class planets capable of supporting life.
However, should it have fallen into the wrong hands, the life-giving properties of the Genesis Device could also be regarded as a doomsday machine. Due to its indiscriminate nature, the Genesis Device would initiate the Genesis Effect on any given space body, occupied or otherwise. Following Khan Noonien Singh’s assault on Regula I, he soon came into possession of its prototype. With the Genesis Device in his possession, Khan intended to use this weapon of mass destruction in order to exert control over his enemies, and instill fear into the hearts of those who would oppose him.

In the early 22nd century, the Vulcan High Command initiated the Interspecies Medical Exchange. Also known as the “IME”, this initiative involved the collaboration of governments hailing from both the Alpha and Beta Quadrants. Its primary purpose was to facilitate the exchange of xenobiological information between a multitude of species. Noting a close collaboration between Human and Denobulan medical personnel, their respective governments established Cold Station 12 as a unified medical research facility capable of expediting further collaboration between both species. Although Cold Station 12 housed a number viral samples dangerous to both Humans and Denobulans alike, the installation was also home to more than 1,800 embryos of genetically-engineered superhumans known as Augments - a sinister relic of the Eugenics Wars that ravaged Earth in the late 20th century.
During Cold Station 12’s formative years, Doctor Arik Soong served as the station’s senior medical director. An Augment sympathizer, Doctor Soong believed that humanity committed a grave mistake when they abandoned the concept of genetic engineering following the conclusion of the Eugenics Wars. Absconding from his post with nineteen Augment embryos housed in their stasis globes, Doctor Soong believed that he could raise these Augments as his own children. His ultimate goal was to prove that genetic engineering was a noble endeavor, and that tyrants such as Khan Noonien Singh were the exception rather than the rule.

The Omega Molecule is widely regarded as one of the most volatile, potentially destructive substances existing in the observable universe. This highly unstable molecule was almost impossible to regulate via conventional methods. Upon destabilization, the Omega Molecule was capable of destroying all known subspace within an indeterminate radius. Although theorized to be a naturally occurring molecule with origins spanning back as far as the Big Bang itself, it was possible to synthesize the Omega Molecule via the utilization of boronite ore. One such species who were known to have successfully synthesized an Omega Molecule was the Borg, who referred to it as “Particle 010”.
Following on from intel acquired by U.S.S. Voyager during their time in the Delta Quadrant, enterprising Independents found themselves capable of stabilizing the Omega Molecule by tuning their containment fields to match the Omega Molecule’s resonance frequency of 1.68 terahertz. The result of taking such a risk is acquiring one of the most potentially destructive weapons in the known galaxy - an irresistible prospect for any Independent operator.

Capable of surviving some of the most barren, hostile conditions in known existence, the Ceti Eel was one of the few recognized indigenous species found on Ceti Alpha V. As a burrowing sand-crawler, the Ceti Eel managed to endure the desolation of its homeworld caused by the destruction of the neighboring planet, Ceti Alpha VI. The larvae of the Ceti Eel were highly parasitic in nature. Once capable of doing so, the larva would seek out and enter the ear canal of any nearby larger mammal. Deep within the recesses of the brain, nestled firmly around the cerebral cortex, the Ceti Eel would quickly devour the inhibitions of its host, rendering them highly susceptible to the suggestions of others. Upon reaching full maturity, the adult Ceti Eel’s presence would inevitably lead to the painful demise of its host.
When Khan Noonien Singh and his loyal Augment disciples were exiled to Ceti Alpha V, a number of them fell victim to the Ceti Eel, including Khan’s beloved wife. These events would ignite a burning fury in the heart of Khan, a fury that would prove parasitic in its own right. When the U.S.S. Reliant arrived on Ceti Alpha V, Khan was quick to recognize Commander Chekov as one of those responsible for the incarceration of his people. Understanding that this chance meeting proved to be an opportunity to inflict pain on those who had so deeply wounded him, Khan weaponized the Ceti Eel against Commander Chekov and his captain. Consequently, by commandeering the U.S.S. Reliant, Khan was granted a gift that until now, he could have only dreamed of - the opportunity to exact revenge on James T. Kirk, and the means by which to find him.

The Romulan Star Empire is well-known for securing its borders from prying eyes. With multiple levels of security running like clockwork, it’s unlikely a stray ship will ever stumble upon Romulan territory. However, if one managed to pass the cloaked scouts and avoid lurking Scimitars, it will have to face Romulan Mines.
These spheroids filled with tricobalt explosives can deal devastating damage to unsuspecting vessels, detonating into a thermokinetic explosion. Generally used to guard the borders of the Empire, the Mines are hidden from radars with the help of cloaking technology, and attach themselves to the enemy hulls upon contact, ensuring that nobody daring to sneak onto the Empire’s grounds leaves unscathed.

Terok Nor has been an integral part of the Bajoran occupation, and losing it meant losing control over Bajor, so it was only a matter of time before the Cardassians would develop a program to ensure their ownership of the station. The resulting device was a preprogrammed counterinsurgency system ready to manage critical situations, from initiating the station's lockdown to filling the habitat ring with neurocine gas.
Installed initially by Dukat to consolidate his rule over the station even in the event of his incapacitation, the device, unbeknownst to him, was also designed to stop him from ever leaving Terok Nor. The last level of defense included the station's self-destruction, but should Dukat attempt to abandon it, he would learn that all access and deactivation codes have been revoked - if Terok Nor goes down, everyone aboard it should too.

Small enough to stay invisible to the sensors, but powerful and numerous enough to stop the Dominion invasion, Self-Replicating Mines were an ingenious invention protecting the Alpha Quadrant from vast enemy forces. Created by Miles O'Brien, Jadzia Dax, and Rom, these tiny and powerful weapons could replicate their neighbors should they detonate or be destroyed, thus acting as an explosive impervious net, stopping anything passing through it.

Genetically engineered pathogens make the best mass destruction weapon, requiring limited resources for lethal results. Harvester is one such invention, created to penetrate the skin of the potential host to disrupt their genes and annihilate the whole planet's population. 
However, when the two warring factions utilizing this pathogen found peace, a new challenge arose: Harvester was too genetically perfect to be destroyed or simply neutralized. After many attempts, the Federation was involved, and it was up to Doctor Bashir to find a solution to this predicament. And now, when most of the Harvester has been rendered safe, any knowledge leading to its recreation could endanger millions of people.

The Syndicate leaves no traces in its business. And if even one appears, it is quickly taken care of using the best clandestine technology available in the underworld. Orion Syndicate bombs with proximity field generators are made to swiftly eliminate threats and destroy incriminating evidence. These bombs are easy to construct and place into the target's vehicles, and are impossible to trace after detonation.
However, due to its compact size, the Orion Syndicate bomb can only do so much damage, and its placement plays a critical role. It is up to elusive Syndicate agents to place the explosives near the key systems to disable life support, communication, and transportation modules, leaving their victims vulnerable and powerless.

Bio-mimetic Gel is a synthetic substance with a myriad of potential applications. Its most common uses are within the realms of medical science, genetic studies and even advanced bioweapon research. Essentially, it is designed to replicate the properties and functions of living biological tissues. Although this unusual substance serves a multitude of purposes, it is considered to be highly-regulated contraband within the Federation's jurisdiction. Widely regarded as a volatile material, bio-mimetic gel can be potentially hazardous if not transported under appropriate conditions. All Commanders are advised to exercise extreme caution when handling or transporting this substance.

Particle synthesis is a highly-advanced technology that enables its users to create artificial structures and environments that would be considered far more convincing than those produced through typical holo-imaging. In prior applications, it has even been known to completely alter the visual appearance of a starship. In the hands of a cunning Commander, particle synthesis could be used to gain the upper hand in battle by lulling enemies into thinking that they are among allies.

Typically found on other Starfleet vessels such as the Constitution II and Intrepid classes, the U.S.S. Reliant has been fitted with Mark VI Photon Torpedoes in order to bolster its effectiveness in the battlefield. At the behest of Captain James T. Kirk, a Mark VI Photon Torpedo casing was also used as a makeshift coffin for the late Commander Spock, following his valiant sacrifice at the conclusion of the Battle of the Mutara Nebula.

The Portable Transwarp Beaming Device was a unique type of personal transporter, designed to make use of the transwarp beaming equation that was postulated by the renowned Starfleet engineer Montgomery Scott. Now embedded into the warp drive configuration of the U.S.S. Vengeance, this daunting vessel is now capable of achieving transwarp speeds that far exceed a typical vessel's warp drive. The U.S.S. Vengeance pursues its prey with ruthless efficiency, ensuring that there is no corner of the galaxy safe from its reach.

A multitronic computer system developed by Dr Richard Daystrom, as an improvement over his earlier duotronic designs. It manifested as a sophisticated artificial intelligence, supposedly capable of running the operation of an entire starship by itself. However, during the war games of its trial run, the computer exhibited dangerous and erratic behavior, leading to many deaths. It was revealed later that to achieve his dream of a fully-automated artificial intelligence, Daystrom had encoded his own memory engrams into the device, resulting in the computer also inheriting the nervous breakdown he was undergoing.

Developed during the Dominion War by the Obsidian Order of the Cardassian Union, the Stasis Field Generator proved highly effective at forcing Changelings to remain in humanoid form, making it suitable for interrogations. The device inflicted horrendous pain on the victim, and would even cause them to lose molecular cohesion, since they were unable to regenerate - as witnessed when Elim Garak tested one such device on Odo in 2371.

A highly-sophisticated computer, able to process and store the vast calculations necessary for a starship to operate a transwarp drive, at a wide variety of transwarp factors. Such a system was installed on the prototype U.S.S. Excelsior as part of Starfleet’s modernization efforts. However, the experiment appeared to have failed when the computer, believed over-engineered by some, proved incredibly easy to sabotage - via the removal of three small components.

Created by Dr Tristan Adams as a form of therapy for prisoners with mental illness, the device was subsequently turned on all patients and staff, with a view to furthering the doctor’s understanding of the technology. The Neural Neutralizer worked by rendering the patient highly susceptible to suggestion, dependent on various factors, such as the intensity of the beam emitter and the patient’s willpower. However, long-term or repeated exposure could cause permanent synaptic damage, as well as a lethal “emptying” effect on the mind.

Created by Dr Tristan Adams as a form of therapy for prisoners with mental illness, the device was subsequently turned on all patients and staff, with a view to furthering the doctor’s understanding of the technology. The Neural Neutralizer worked by rendering the patient highly susceptible to suggestion, dependent on various factors, such as the intensity of the beam emitter and the patient’s willpower. However, long-term or repeated exposure could cause permanent synaptic damage, as well as a lethal “emptying” effect on the mind.

A handheld device used within a Cardassian interrogation chamber. The actuator is designed to remotely activate a torture device implanted in the prisoner’s chest, causing excruciating pain even on the lowest setting. Captain Picard was subject to such a device during his capture and internment on Celtris III in 2369, when Gul Madred failed to extract information from him about the Federation’s defenses in the Minos Korva system. Madred also failed in using the actuator to break Picard’s will, when asking him to state that he could see five floodlights in the interrogation chamber when there were only four.

Tan Ru was a sophisticated alien space probe of unknown origin, which was programmed to collect and sterilize soil samples, likely for colonization purposes. However, when it encountered the Earth probe Nomad in deep space, it underwent a transformation. Nomad, which had been dispatched to seek out new life, had become damaged when it collided with a meteoroid. Directives from Tan Ru transferred to Nomad, creating a semi-sentient machine lifeform, whose new directive was to find “imperfect” life and sterilize it. The crew of the U.S.S. Enterprise under Captain Kirk encountered the transformed entity in 2267, following its decimation of the Malurian system’s inhabitants. It caused havoc aboard the ship, including killing and then later “repairing” Chief Engineer Montgomery Scott, bringing him back to life. The probe was planning to head for Earth next, but self-destructed when Captain Kirk convinced it that it, too, was imperfect.
